title: Google SecOps Detection Alerts (using Azure Functions)
descriptionMarkdown:
The [Google SecOps](https://cloud.google.com/security/products/security-operations) (formerly Chronicle) data connector provides the capability to ingest detection alerts from Google SecOps into Microsoft Sentinel using the [legacyStreamDetectionAlerts](https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/reference/rest/v1alpha/projects.locations.instances.legacy/legacyStreamDetectionAlerts) API.

## Architecture

This connector uses a **two-function polling design**:

1. **GoogleSecOpsToStorage**: Polls the Google SecOps API on a configurable schedule and saves raw detection batches to Azure File Share
2. **AzureStorageToSentinel**: Monitors the file share and ingests saved detections into Microsoft Sentinel via the Logs Ingestion API (DCR)

Key features:
- **Real-time polling**: Configurable schedule (default: every 10 minutes)
- **Checkpoint management**: Tracks pagination tokens and time windows to prevent data loss
- **Automatic lookback**: On first run, pulls data from N days ago (configurable 1-7 days, default 1)
- **Streaming response handling**: Handles large API responses with per-message timeout detection
- **Raw data preservation**: Saves complete API responses for audit and reprocessing
- **Sentinel DCR integration**: Posts detection alerts to custom table via Azure Monitor Ingestion API

**Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:**

This solution depends on the following technologies, and some of which may be in [Preview](https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/preview-supplemental-terms/) state or may incur additional ingestion or operational costs:

- [Azure Functions](https://azure.microsoft.com/services/functions/) (Python 3.12 runtime)
- [Azure File Share](https://azure.microsoft.com/services/storage/files/) (for checkpoint and raw data storage)
- [Azure Monitor Ingestion API](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/logs-ingestion-api-overview) (DCR-based)
- [Azure Key Vault](https://azure.microsoft.com/services/key-vault/) (optional for credential storage)

sampleQueries.query:
GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
 | sort by detection_time desc
 | take 10

GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
 | summarize Count=count() by severity
 | sort by Count desc

GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
 | where alert_state == 'ALERTING'
 | summarize Count=count() by rule_name
 | sort by Count desc
 | take 10

GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
 | extend Hour = bin(detection_time, 1h)
 | summarize Count=count() by Hour, severity
 | render columnchart

dataTypes.name: GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(30d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions on the workspace are required.
Write permissions to Data Collection Rules are required.
Write permissions to Data Collection Endpoints are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Storage Account for File Share operations are required.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra ID App Registration
customs.description: An app registration with API permissions for Azure Monitor Ingestion is required for posting data to Sentinel.

customs.name: Google Cloud Service Account
customs.description: A Google Cloud service account with Chronicle API access is required. The service account JSON key must be provided during deployment. [See the documentation to learn more about Google SecOps API access](https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/reference/rest).

instructionSteps.title: Information
instructionSteps.description:
>**NOTE:** This connector uses two Azure Functions to connect to the Google SecOps API:

- **GoogleSecOpsToStorage**: Polls the Google SecOps API on a schedule and saves raw detection batches to Azure File Share
- **AzureStorageToSentinel**: Monitors the file share and ingests saved detections into Microsoft Sentinel

This architecture ensures zero data loss with checkpoint management and prevents duplicate ingestion.

instructionSteps.title: Cost Warning
instructionSteps.description:
>**NOTE:** This connector uses Azure Functions and Azure Storage, which may result in additional costs. Review pricing:
- [Azure Functions pricing](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/)
- [Azure Storage pricing](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/storage/blobs/)

instructionSteps.title: Optional: Secure Credentials
instructionSteps.description: >**(Optional Step)** Securely store the Google service account JSON in Azure Key Vault. [Follow these instructions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references) to use Azure Key Vault with an Azure Function App.

instructionSteps.title: Google SecOps Configuration
instructionSteps.description:
**Step 1: Set up Google SecOps Service Account**

1. Ensure you have a Google SecOps (Chronicle) instance
2. Go to the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select the project associated with your Google SecOps instance
3. Navigate to **IAM & Admin > Service Accounts** and click **Create Service Account**
4. Enter a name (e.g., `chronicle-api-sa`) and click **Create and Continue**
5. Assign a role with permission: `chronicle.legacies.legacyStreamDetectionAlerts`
6. Click **Create key** > **JSON** to download the service account credentials
7. Note down your **Chronicle Project ID**, **Region**, and **Instance ID** from the Google SecOps console

instructionSteps.title: Azure App Registration
instructionSteps.description:
**Step 2: Create App Registration for Sentinel Ingestion**

1. Sign in to the [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/)
2. Search for and select **Microsoft Entra ID**
3. Under **Manage**, select **App registrations > New registration**
4. Enter a display **Name** (e.g., `gcs-detection-alerts-app`)
5. Select **Register**
6. Record the **Application (client) ID** and **Tenant ID**
7. Under **Certificates & secrets**, create a **New client secret** and save the value
8. Under **API permissions**, add **Monitoring.Write** for Azure Monitor Ingestion

instructionSteps.title: Sentinel Configuration
instructionSteps.description:
**Step 3: Get Sentinel Resources**

1. In the Azure portal, go to your **Log Analytics workspace**
2. Note the **Workspace name** and **Resource group**
3. Go to **Access control (IAM)** and note your **Azure Entra Object ID** (User ID)
4. These will be used during function deployment

instructionSteps.title: Deploy Function App
instructionSteps.description:
**Step 4: Deploy Azure Function App**

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below
2. Fill in the deployment parameters:
   - **FunctionName**: Prefix for your function app (1-11 chars)
   - **Location**: Region for deployment
   - **WorkspaceName**: Your Log Analytics workspace name
   - **ChronicleProjectId**: Your Google SecOps project ID
   - **ChronicleRegion**: Your Google SecOps region (default: `us`)
   - **ChronicleInstanceId**: Your Google SecOps instance ID
   - **ChronicleServiceAccountJson**: Paste the full service account JSON key
   - **AzureClientId**: App registration client ID
   - **AzureClientSecret**: App registration client secret
   - **AzureEntraObjectID**: Your Azure Entra user/app object ID
   - **TenantId**: Your Azure Entra tenant ID
   - **LookbackDays**: Days to look back on first run (1-7, default: 1)
   - **DetectionBatchSize**: Detections per API request (default: 1000)
   - **MaxDetections**: Max detections per API response (default: 100)
   - **Schedule**: Timer trigger CRON expression (default: `0 */10 * * * *` = every 10 minutes)
3. Click **Review + Create** and then **Create**

instructionSteps.title: Verify Ingestion
instructionSteps.description:
**Step 5: Verify Data Ingestion**

1. After deployment completes (5-10 minutes), go to your Log Analytics workspace
2. Run this query to verify data is arriving:
   ```kusto
   GCSDetectionAlerts_CL
   | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
   | summarize Count=count()
   ```
3. Check the Azure Function App **Monitor** tab to verify function executions
4. If no data appears, check:
   - Function app logs for errors
   - Service account permissions in Google Cloud
   - App registration permissions in Azure
